turns.content: What brings you in today?
Well, my wife’s been on my case. It’s been about three years since my last physical, so she wants me to get everything checked out. Blood pressure, cholesterol, the whole deal. I feel fine, though.
When did this start?
My wife’s been bringing it up for a few months now. We finally scheduled the appointment last week.
Can you describe the pain or discomfort? Where exactly do you feel it?
Honestly, there’s no pain or discomfort. That’s why I keep telling my wife I’m fine. Nothing hurts, no aches, no complaints.
Have you had any fever or chills?
No, no fever or chills. I haven’t been sick at all.
What medications are you currently taking?
I’m not on any prescription medications. I take a multivitamin every now and then, but that’s about it.
Do you have any chronic medical conditions?
No, not that I know of. Nothing's ever come up at previous checkups. I keep pretty active with work, so I figure that helps.
Are you allergic to any medications?
Not that I'm aware of. I’ve never had a reaction to anything before.
Have you tried anything to relieve the symptoms?
Like I said, I don’t really have any symptoms to treat. Nothing hurts, nothing’s off. So I haven’t tried anything.
Does anything make it better or worse?
There's nothing to make better or worse, because there's nothing going on. I just came in for the routine checkup.
How has this been affecting your daily life?
It’s not affecting anything at all. I go to work, manage the crew, come home, sleep fine. No issues.
Do you smoke or drink alcohol?
I don't smoke. I'll have a beer maybe once a week with dinner, but nothing heavy.
Has anyone in your family had similar problems?
Not really. My dad had a heart attack in his late 50s, but he also smoked and didn't take care of himself. Nothing on my mom's side that I know of.
Have you experienced anything like this before?
No, never. This is just a routine checkup, nothing unusual has ever come up.
On a scale of 1 to 10, how would you rate what you're going through right now — physically, and also emotionally?
Physically, I'd say a solid 1. There's nothing wrong. Emotionally, probably a 1 or 2 — I'm not worried about anything. Just here because my wife insisted.
Is there anything else you think I should know?
No, that's about it. I think you’ve covered everything. Just the usual blood work and numbers would do me fine.
And how have you been coping with all of this?
There's nothing to cope with, really. I just go about my day and it's business as usual. No stress or worry about it.
And how have you been coping with all of this?
Same as before — there's nothing to cope with. I'm not stressed, not losing sleep, not changing anything. Life goes on like normal.
And how have you been coping with all of this?
I've told you — there's nothing to cope with. I feel fine, no issues, no stress. I'm just here for the tests my wife wanted me to get.
And how have you been coping with all of this?
I appreciate you asking, but my answer's the same as before. There's nothing going on to cope with. I'm fine.
And how have you been coping with all of this?
Look, I think we're going in circles a bit. I’m not coping with anything because there’s nothing wrong. Can we just move on to the blood pressure and cholesterol checks?
